Building Dreams, Restoring Homes

Horizon General Contractor brings quality craftsmanship to Salt Lake Valley. Trusted for over a decade, we handle everything from historic bungalow restorations to modern condo remodels.

What We Do

Our Services

SEE ALL SERVICES →

Who We Are

Rooted in Salt Lake, Built for You

Founded in 2012, Horizon General Contractor began with a simple mission: to bring honest, quality construction to our community. What started as a two-person operation has grown into a team of skilled craftsmen serving the entire Wasatch Front.

We approach every project with the care it deserves, whether it's preserving the character of a 1920s Avenues bungalow or updating a Sugar House ranch home for modern living. Our team takes pride in solving the unique challenges posed by Utah's climate and housing stock.

As locals ourselves, we're committed to giving back to Salt Lake City. From volunteering at Habitat for Humanity builds to sponsoring Little League teams in Draper, we believe in building up our community as much as we build homes.

12+ Years
800+ Projects
97% Satisfaction
GET IN TOUCH →

Our Difference

Why Choose Horizon General Contractor

Climate-Smart Construction

We specialize in solutions for Utah's freeze-thaw cycles, heavy snow loads, and dry air that can wreak havoc on homes.

Historic Home Experts

From original hardwood restoration in The Avenues to proper brick repointing on Capitol Hill, we preserve your home's character.

Full-Service Convenience

We handle everything from permits to final inspections, making your project smooth from start to finish.

Local Materials

We source materials from Utah suppliers whenever possible, supporting local businesses and reducing environmental impact.

Ready to Transform Your Space?

Schedule a free consultation to discuss your project with our Salt Lake City team.

Get a Free Estimate Call 555-gen-r90

Client Feedback

What Our Clients Say

“

Horizon transformed our 1960s Sugar House kitchen into a modern masterpiece while keeping the mid-century charm we love. They suggested local quartzite counters that handle our busy family and Utah's temperature swings beautifully. The team was respectful of our home and finished two days ahead of schedule.

Megan T. Kitchen Remodeling
“

We needed more space for our growing family but didn't want to leave our Avenues neighborhood. Horizon designed a second-story addition that looks like it's always been part of our 1920s bungalow. They navigated historic district approvals seamlessly and reinforced the foundation for earthquake safety - crucial in our area.

James K. Home Addition
“

After years of hard water stains in our Draper home, we finally have a bathroom that stands up to Utah's water quality. Horizon installed a whole-house water softener and chose materials that resist mineral buildup. The heated floors make winter mornings bearable, and the shower's ventilation keeps moisture under control.

Sarah L. Bathroom Remodeling
“

The team at Horizon replaced our crumbling siding with fiber cement that looks like wood but handles Salt Lake's weather much better. They repaired our roof's ice dam damage properly and installed oversized gutters for spring runoff. Our Capitol Hill home looks better than ever and is finally protected from the elements.

David P. Exterior Remodeling
“

Our Sandy basement was a damp storage space until Horizon transformed it into a cozy family room. They addressed moisture issues we didn't even know we had and installed a radon mitigation system for safety. The space stays perfectly dry even during heavy spring rains, and the built-in shelving makes great use of awkward angles.

Rachel M. Basement Finishing
“

Our new Horizon-built deck is the perfect spot for summer evenings with views of the Wasatch Mountains. They designed it to handle heavy snow loads in winter and built in shade structures for hot summer days. The composite material still looks new after a full year with zero maintenance - exactly what we wanted for our busy Provo household.

Thomas R. Deck Construction

Our Portfolio

Our Recent Work

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

How do you handle lead paint in older Salt Lake homes?
We're EPA-certified in lead-safe practices and always test materials in pre-1978 homes. Containment procedures protect your family during renovation.
Can you work within historic district guidelines?
Yes, we've completed projects in Salt Lake's historic districts and understand approval processes with the Historic Landmark Commission.
